title: Ukraine's Missile Problem During Middle East War
summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky says Ukraine might not have enough missiles to fight Russia because of the war in the Middle East. In an interview with the BBC, he explains that Russia's leader, Vladimir Putin, wants a long war between the US, Israel, and Iran because it helps Russia. When the US sends missiles to the Middle East, Ukraine gets fewer. Zelensky says this is bad for Ukraine because it makes peace talks harder and energy prices higher. He also talks about US President Donald Trump, who wants to help end the war but doesn't take sides. Zelensky hopes Trump and UK leader Sir Keir Starmer can work together to support Ukraine. The Middle East war has used many missiles quickly, and Ukraine worries about running out.

questions.question: Why does Ukraine face a missile shortage?
options: Because of the Middle East war
Because the US stopped making missiles
questions.correct_answer: Because of the Middle East war

What does Zelensky hope Trump and Starmer will do?
How does the Middle East war affect energy prices?
What is a challenge for Ukraine according to Zelensky?
options: No war in the Middle East

questions.question: What does Zelensky say about peace talks?

background_read: Ukraine is a country in Europe fighting a war against Russia, which invaded in 2022. The Middle East war involves countries like the US, Israel, and Iran fighting each other. Missiles are important weapons in modern wars, and countries like the US make them to help allies. When wars happen in different places, it can be hard for countries to share resources like missiles. Leaders like Zelensky, Putin, Trump, and Starmer make decisions that affect these wars and peace efforts.
Article_Structure: WHO: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky and other leaders like Vladimir Putin, Donald Trump, and Sir Keir Starmer
WHAT: Ukraine might run out of missiles because the Middle East war uses up US supplies, making it harder for Ukraine to fight Russia and delaying peace talks
WHERE: In Ukraine and the Middle East, as discussed in a BBC interview
WHY: To explain how global conflicts are connected and urge leaders to cooperate to support Ukraine

perspectives.description: He is worried that the Middle East war hurts Ukraine by reducing missile supplies and raising energy prices, and he wants Western leaders to unite to help.
